Patch Set 3:

Do we really want to do this?
The task arg was removed from force_sig() for a reason.
Why can't we just send the signal, and let the app handle it.
And if we want to app to DIE DIE DIE, why not send SIGKILL?

At the very least, the answers to these questions should be added as a comment.

Abandoned

in favor of https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/51470

parents.subject: LU-16399 tests: add subtests setup/cleanup records

commit.subject: LU-16893 libcfs: cfs_force_sig deadklock on send_sig
commit.message:
LU-16893 libcfs: cfs_force_sig deadklock on send_sig

When force_sig HAVE_FORCE_SIG_WITH_TASK is not available
cfs_force_sig uses send_sig to duplicate the functionality,
however send_sig also locks siglock, which can trigger
a deadlock and an rcu stall.

cfs_force_sig(sig, task)
  -> spin_lock_irqsave(&task->sighand->siglock, flags);
    -> send_sig(.., task,..)
     -> send_sig_info(.., task)
       -> do_send_sig_info(..., task)
         -> lock_task_sighand(task)
           -> __lock_task_sighand(task, flags)
              {
                  sighand = rcu_dereference(task->sighand);
                  spin_lock_irqsave(&sighand->siglock, *flags);
                  ...
              } 

cfs_force_sig must drop the lock on siglock before calling
send_sig.

HPE-bug-id: LUS-11670
Fixes: db9f9543ec ("LU-12634 libcfs: force_sig() removed task parameter")
Signed-off-by: Shaun Tancheff <shaun.tancheff@hpe.com>
Change-Id: I71c6bb056f97eebad2c7d905c7fb6a2c09f7774d
